links.targetTitle: PSS provides 1-to-1 asymmetrically encrypted messaging with 2–10 second latency on Swarm.
PSS provides 1-to-1 asymmetrically encrypted messaging with 2–10 second latency on Swarm.

global.title: Typing indicators were cut because PSS message latency makes the hint pointless.
global.content:
## Decision (2026-04-18)

The typing indicator, presence dots, and delivery/read receipts — all designed to ride on the GSOC notification rail — were **scratched** before shipping.

## Rationale

- PSS message latency is **2–10 seconds**, dominated by Trojan chunk mining.
- A 'User is typing…' hint is only useful when it arrives faster than the message itself, previewing imminent delivery.
- With PSS, by the time the hint fires and the user reads it, the message is usually already delivered — or still 5s away, at which point the hint provides no information.
- The UX payoff does not justify the complexity of the typing/debounce/stopped-typing state machine.

## What happened to the GSOC plumbing

The adapter code for GSOC notifications is **shipped and working** — see `src/chat/gsoc-notifier.ts`. It's being repurposed for live collaboration (Phase 4): doc-update pings, collab-join/leave events, and cursor broadcasts, all of which are what sub-second notifications were really for. The chat-UI-only consumers are parked.

## Lesson

Latency floors dictate which UI hints make sense. Before designing a 'fast signal' feature on top of a slow transport, check whether the signal's utility survives the transport's latency.
global.description: A 'User is typing…' hint only pays off when it arrives faster than the message; PSS delivers on 2–10s, slower than any useful typing hint, so the indicator was scratched.
